DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT LYON, PHIL AN INDIVIDUAL LYON, BARBARA AN INDIVIDUAL HEARING SET FOR AUG-14-2009 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PHIL LYONS, AND, BARBARA LYONS DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OVERRULED. TEN DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. PLAINTIFFS PLEAD THAT THEY WERE TENANTS, WHICH FOR PURPOSES OF DEMURRER THE COURT ACCEPTS AS TRUE. CHAN VS. ANTEPENKO (1988) 203 CAL.APP. 3D SUPP.21 IS DISTINGUISHABLE. (SEE SPINKS VS. EQUITY RESIDENTIAL BRIARWOOD APARTMENTS (2009) 171 CAL.APP. 4TH 1004, 1041-1042.) FACTUAL ISSUES REMAIN WHETHER ROTTWEILER WAS BRANDISHED IN A MENACING MANNER. PLAINTIFF'S DO NOT ALLEGE THAT THEY FORMALLY RELINQUISHED POSSESSION, BUT RATHER REMOVED THEIR BELONGINGS IN RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S DEMAND FOR POSSESSION. THEREFORE, CC SECTION 789.3 CLAIM PROPER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc And Mtn To Strike Portion Of X-Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANTS ELENA LOKSHINA, and, GERBERT KORYTNY Motion to Strike Portion (request for attorneys' fees) of Cross-Complaint. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. No opposition filed. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Maria Lertora, CSR #11935

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-22-2009 Continued To Nov-20-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PHIL LYONS, and, BARBARA LYONS DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: David Foran, Esq., Law Office of Joseph Sacramento (415) 775-0891, appearing for Defendants and moving party; Hannah R. Salassi, of Lvovich & Volchegursky LLP (415) 392-2560 appearing for Plaintiffs and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled. Ten days leave to answer. Plaintiffs plead that they were tenants, which for purposes of demurrer the court accepts as true. Chan vs. Antepenko (1988) 203 Cal.App. 3d Supp.21 is distinguishable. (See Spinks vs. Equity Residential Briarwood Apartments (2009) 171 Cal.App. 4th 1004, 1041-1042.) Factual issues remain whether Rottweiler was brandished in a menacing manner. Plaintiff's do not allege that they formally relinquished possession, but rather removed their belongings in response to plaintiff's demand for possession. Therefore, CC section 789.3 claim proper. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
